---
title: "Lung scATAC Thesis Workflow"
output: html_document
date: "2025-12-11"
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

```{r}
if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install(c(
  "Seurat", 
  "Signac", 
  "ggplot2", 
  "patchwork", 
  "EnsDb.Mmusculus.v79", 
  "scDblFinder"
))
```

```{r}
set.seed(1234)
library(Signac)
library(Seurat)
library(EnsDb.Mmusculus.v79)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(scDblFinder)
library(harmony)
library(Matrix)
library(BiocGenerics) # Needed for genome annotations
```



```{r}
# Prepare Genome Annotations
# extract gene annotations from EnsDb
annotations <- GetGRangesFromEnsDb(ensdb = EnsDb.Mmusculus.v79)
# change to UCSC style since the data was mapped to mm10
seqlevelsStyle(annotations) <- 'UCSC'
genome(annotations) <- "mm10"
```
```{r}
# Data Loading & Per-Sample Processing
# Define sample information
samples <- data.frame(
  folder = c("CL100168054_L01", "CL100167942_L01", 
             "CL100168054_L02", "CL100167942_L02", 
             "CL100168078_L02", "CL100168078_L01"),
  sample_id = c("Control_F2", "Control_M1", 
                "Case_F3", "Case_M2", "Case_F1", "Case_M3"),
  group = c("Control", "Control", "Case", "Case", "Case", "Case"),
  sex = c("Female", "Male", "Female", "Male", "Female", "Male")
)

base_dir <- "/QRISdata/Q8448/Mouse_disease_data/Lung/Lung_cellatac"
obj_list <- list()

# Start loop
for (i in 1:nrow(samples)) {
  cur_folder <- samples$folder[i]
  cur_id <- samples$sample_id[i]
  
  print(paste0("[", i, "/", nrow(samples), "] Processing: ", cur_id))

  # Define paths
  matrix_dir <- file.path(base_dir, cur_folder, "outs", "filter_peak_matrix")
  meta_path <- file.path(base_dir, cur_folder, "outs", "singlecell.csv")
  frag_path <- file.path(base_dir, cur_folder, "outs", "fragments.tsv.gz")
  
  # --- 1. Manually Read Data ---
  counts_mat <- readMM(file = file.path(matrix_dir, "matrix.mtx.gz"))
  barcodes <- read.table(file = file.path(matrix_dir, "barcodes.tsv.gz"), header = FALSE)
  peaks <- read.table(file = file.path(matrix_dir, "peaks.bed.gz"), header = FALSE)
  
  colnames(counts_mat) <- barcodes$V1
  peak_names <- paste0(peaks$V1, ":", peaks$V2, "-", peaks$V3)
  rownames(counts_mat) <- make.unique(peak_names)

  if(file.exists(meta_path)){
      metadata <- read.csv(meta_path, header = TRUE, row.names = 1)
  } else {
      metadata <- NULL
  }

  # --- 2. Create Object ---
  chrom_assay <- CreateChromatinAssay(
    counts = counts_mat,
    sep = c(":", "-"),
    genome = 'mm10',
    fragments = frag_path,
    min.cells = 10,
    min.features = 200,
    annotation = annotations # Add annotation here directly
  )
  
  cur_obj <- CreateSeuratObject(
    counts = chrom_assay,
    assay = "peaks",
    meta.data = metadata, 
    project = cur_id
  )
  
  # Add Metadata
  cur_obj$SampleID <- cur_id
  cur_obj$Group <- samples$group[i]
  cur_obj$Sex <- samples$sex[i]
  cur_obj$Dataset <- "Lung"
  
  # --- 3. Calculate QC Metrics (Per Sample) ---
  print(paste("  - Calculating QC metrics for", cur_id))
  
  # Nucleosome Signal
  cur_obj <- NucleosomeSignal(object = cur_obj)
  
  # TSS Enrichment (Requires annotation added above)
  cur_obj <- TSSEnrichment(object = cur_obj, fast = FALSE)
  
  # Fix nCounts name if needed (optional, Seurat uses nCount_peaks by default)
  cur_obj$peak_region_fragments <- cur_obj$nCount_peaks

  # --- 4. Doublet Detection (Per Sample) ---
  # Running scDblFinder on individual sample is scientifically more accurate
  print(paste("  - Running scDblFinder for", cur_id))
  
  # Use the counts matrix for doublet detection
  # We select only cells present in the Seurat object to ensure match
  mtx <- GetAssayData(cur_obj, layer = "counts")
  dbl_results <- scDblFinder(mtx, verbose = FALSE)
  
  cur_obj$scDblFinder.class <- dbl_results$scDblFinder.class
  cur_obj$scDblFinder.score <- dbl_results$scDblFinder.score
  
  # 5. FILTERING INSIDE THE LOOP (The key change)
  # We remove bad cells NOW, before merging.
  print(paste("  - Filtering", cur_id, "..."))
  print(paste("    Before:", ncol(cur_obj)))
  
  cur_obj <- subset(
    x = cur_obj,
    subset = nCount_peaks > 1000 &       # Paper: filterFrags > 1000
             nCount_peaks < 100000 &     # Remove extreme outliers
             TSS.enrichment > 4 &        # Paper: filterTSS > 4
             nucleosome_signal < 4 &     # Standard QC
             scDblFinder.class == "singlet" # Remove doublets
  )
  
  print(paste("    After:", ncol(cur_obj)))
  
  obj_list[[i]] <- cur_obj
}
```

```{r}
# Merging and Flobal Filtering
print("Merging samples...")
lung <- merge(
  x = obj_list[[1]],
  y = obj_list[2:length(obj_list)],
  add.cell.ids = samples$sample_id
)

# Clean up memory
rm(obj_list)
gc()

print("Dimensions BEFORE filtering:")
print(dim(lung))
```

```{r}
VlnPlot(
  object = lung,
  features = c('nCount_peaks', 'TSS.enrichment', 'nucleosome_signal'),
  ncol = 3,
  pt.size = 0,
  group.by = "SampleID" 
)
```
```{r}
# Check all available Sample IDs in your object
print(unique(lung$SampleID))
```

```{r}
# Visualize violin plot one by one
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# 1. Set the specific sample name you want to inspect
# (Replace 'Control_M1' with your actual sample ID from the list above)
target_sample <- "Case_M3" 

# 2. Prepare the metrics to plot
# This automatically checks if 'pct_reads_in_peaks' exists to avoid errors
features_to_plot <- c("nCount_peaks", "TSS.enrichment", "nucleosome_signal")

if ("pct_reads_in_peaks" %in% colnames(lung@meta.data)) {
  features_to_plot <- c("pct_reads_in_peaks", features_to_plot)
}

# 3. Plotting
# CRITICAL STEP: Set the identity classes to 'SampleID' so we can select by sample
Idents(lung) <- "SampleID"

VlnPlot(
  object = lung,
  features = features_to_plot,
  idents = target_sample,   # <--- This filters the plot to show ONLY this sample
  ncol = length(features_to_plot),
  pt.size = 0.1,            # Show individual cells as dots
) + 
  plot_annotation(
    title = paste("QC Metrics for Single Sample:", target_sample),
    subtitle = paste("Total Cells:", length(WhichCells(lung, idents = target_sample)))
  )
```

```{r}
# Dimensionality Reduction & Batch Correction

# Standard LSI
lung <- RunTFIDF(lung)
lung <- FindTopFeatures(lung, min.cutoff = 'q0')
lung <- RunSVD(lung)

# Check depth correlation (Optional)
DepthCor(lung)

# Run Harmony (Manual Matrix Method for Seurat v5)
library(harmony)

print("Running Harmony...")
lsi_embeddings <- Embeddings(lung, reduction = "lsi")

harmony_embeddings <- HarmonyMatrix(
  data_mat = lsi_embeddings,
  meta_data = lung@meta.data,
  vars_use = "SampleID",   
  do_pca = FALSE,          
  verbose = TRUE
) 

lung[["harmony"]] <- CreateDimReducObject(
  embeddings = harmony_embeddings,
  key = "harmony_",
  assay = "peaks"  
)
```

```{r}
# Clustering and UMAP
# Run UMAP
lung <- RunUMAP(object = lung, reduction = 'harmony', dims = 2:30, seed.use = 1234)

# Find Neighbors & Clusters
lung <- FindNeighbors(object = lung, reduction = 'harmony', dims = 2:30)
# Resolution 0.8 is what the paper used, but 0.2-0.5 is often cleaner. 
# Stick to what worked for you or try 0.8 to match paper strictly.
lung <- FindClusters(object = lung, verbose = FALSE, algorithm = 3, resolution = 0.8, random.seed = 1234)

# Plot
DimPlot(object = lung, label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.5) + ggtitle("UMAP after Harmony")
```


```{r}
# compute gene activities
gene.activites <- GeneActivity(lung)
# add gene activities as a new assay
lung[['RNA']] <- CreateAssayObject(counts = gene.activites)
lung <- NormalizeData(
  object = lung,
  assay = 'RNA',
  normalization.method = 'LogNormalize',
  scale.factor = median(lung$nCount_RNA)
)
```

```{r}
# 1. Define Lung Marker List based on Figure S5
# Note: Gene names are formatted for Mouse (TitleCase)
marker_list_lung <- list(
  # --- Epithelial Cells ---
  "AT1" = c("Ager"),               # 
  "AT2" = c("Sftpb"),              # 
  "Ciliated" = c("Foxj1"),         # (Common marker, verify if present, otherwise skip)
  
  # --- Stromal / Endothelial ---
  "Fibroblast" = c("Gucy1a1", "Speg", "Cntn5"), # 
  "Endothelial" = c("Vwf", "Clec14a", "Dpep1"), # 
  
  # --- Myeloid (Macrophages/Monocytes) ---
  "Alveolar Macrophage" = c("Mcemp1"),          # 
  "Interstitial Macrophage" = c("C1qb"),        # 
  "Monocytes" = c("Cd14"),                      # 
  "Dendritic Cells" = c("Ccl17", "Itgae"),      # 
  
  # --- Lymphoid (B/T/NK) ---
  "B Cells" = c("Cd79b"),                       # 
  "Plasma/B-Jchain" = c("Jchain"),              # 
  "T Cells (General)" = c("Cd3g"),              # [cite: 853]
  "CD4 T Cells" = c("Cd4"),                     # [cite: 854]
  "CD8 T Cells" = c("Cd8a"),                    # [cite: 855]
  "NK Cells" = c("Nkg7")                        # 
)

# 2. Switch to Gene Activity (RNA) Assay
# Ensure you have run GeneActivity() previously on your ATAC object
# Use 'lung_final' or your current clean object name
DefaultAssay(lung) <- 'RNA' 

# 3. Create the DotPlot
DotPlot(
  object = lung, 
  features = marker_list_lung,
  cols = c("lightgrey", "red"), # Matches the visual style of the PDF
  dot.scale = 8
) + 
  RotatedAxis() + 
  ggtitle("Cell Type Markers (Figure S5)") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)
  )

FeaturePlot(
  object = lung,
  features = c("Sftpb", "Mcemp1", "Cd79b", "Cd3g", "Vwf", "Gucy1a1"),
  min.cutoff = "q9",
  ncol = 3
)

```
```{r}
all.markers <- FindAllMarkers(
  object = lung,
  only.pos = TRUE,    
  min.pct = 0.25,   
  logfc.threshold = 0.25
)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
top5_markers <- all.markers %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_max(n = 5, order_by = avg_log2FC)
print(as.data.frame(top5_markers))
```
```{r}
my_object <- lung
my_object <- ScaleData(my_object, features = top5_markers$gene)
DoHeatmap(my_object, features = top5_markers$gene) + 
  NoLegend() +
  ggtitle("Top 5 Markers per Cluster") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5))
```
```{R}

# Define gene lists
genes_AT1       <- c("Ager", "Hopx", "Pdpn", "Aqp5")
genes_Ciliated  <- c("Foxj1", "Tubb4b")
genes_T_cell    <- c("Trac", "Cd3d", "Cd3e") # Cd3d/e are pan-T markers
genes_ILC2      <- c("Il7r", "Gata3", "Rora", "Areg")
genes_Eosinophil<- c("Epx", "Prg2", "Siglecf") # Note: Siglecf is also expressed in alveolar macrophages
genes_Mac       <- c("Lyz2", "Adgre1", "Marco", "Mrc1") # Adgre1 = F4/80
genes_Epi       <- c("Epcam", "Krt8", "Krt18", "Krt19")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Group 1: Resolving Cluster 4 & 10 (Epithelial/AT1/Ciliated)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
print("Plotting Epithelial Group (AT1 vs Ciliated)...")
p1 <- FeaturePlot(lung, features = c(genes_AT1, genes_Ciliated), 
                  cols = c("lightgrey", "red"), ncol = 3) + ggtitle("AT1 & Ciliated Markers")
p2 <- VlnPlot(lung, features = c(genes_AT1, genes_Ciliated), 
              stack = TRUE, flip = TRUE) + NoLegend()

print(p1)
print(p2)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Group 2: Resolving Cluster 5 (Eosinophil vs ILC2 vs T cell)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
print("Plotting Immune Group (Eosinophil vs ILC2 vs T)...")
# Contrast check: High Epx/Prg2 indicates Eosinophils; High Gata3/Il7r indicates ILC2
p3 <- FeaturePlot(lung, features = c(genes_Eosinophil, genes_ILC2, genes_T_cell), 
                  cols = c("lightgrey", "red"), ncol = 4)
p4 <- VlnPlot(lung, features = c(genes_Eosinophil, genes_ILC2, genes_T_cell), 
              stack = TRUE, flip = TRUE) + NoLegend()

print(p3)
print(p4)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Group 3: Validating Cluster 0 (Macrophage subtypes)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
print("Plotting Macrophage Group...")
p5 <- FeaturePlot(lung, features = genes_Mac, 
                  cols = c("lightgrey", "red"), ncol = 2)
p6 <- VlnPlot(lung, features = genes_Mac, 
              stack = TRUE, flip = TRUE) + NoLegend()

print(p5)
print(p6)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Group 4: General Epithelial Markers (Verify if Clusters 4/10/14 are all epithelial)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
FeaturePlot(lung, features = genes_Epi, cols = c("lightgrey", "red"), ncol = 2)
```
```{r}


# =======================================================
# Validation Group 1: Cluster 4 (T cell vs ILC2)
# =======================================================
# Logic:
# - If Cd3d/Cd3e/Trac are High -> T Cell (ILCs lack surface CD3/TCR)
# - If Cd3 is Negative, but Gata3/Il7r/Areg are High -> ILC2
genes_T     <- c("Trac", "Cd3d", "Cd3e")
genes_ILC2  <- c("Il7r", "Gata3", "Rora", "Areg")

print("Plotting Cluster 4 Check (T vs ILC2)...")
p1 <- VlnPlot(lung, features = c(genes_T, genes_ILC2), 
              stack = TRUE, flip = TRUE, pt.size = 0) + 
      ggtitle("Cluster 4 Check: T vs ILC2")

p2 <- FeaturePlot(lung, features = c("Cd3d", "Gata3", "Areg"), 
                  ncol = 3, cols = c("lightgrey", "red"))

print(p1)
print(p2)

# =======================================================
# Validation Group 2: Cluster 5 (Confirm Eosinophil & Exclude Macrophage)
# =======================================================
# Logic:
# - Eosinophil MUST have: Epx, Prg2, Ear1, Ear2, Alox15
# - MUST NOT have Macrophage markers: Adgre1 (F4/80), Lyz2, Csf1r
# - If Epx is absent but Adgre1 is high -> It is a Macrophage subtype
genes_Eos <- c("Epx", "Prg2", "Ear1", "Ear2", "Alox15")
genes_Mac_Exclude <- c("Adgre1", "Lyz2", "Csf1r")

print("Plotting Cluster 5 Check (Eosinophil vs Macrophage)...")
p3 <- VlnPlot(lung, features = c(genes_Eos, genes_Mac_Exclude), 
              stack = TRUE, flip = TRUE, pt.size = 0) + 
      ggtitle("Cluster 5 Check: Eosinophil Identity")

p4 <- FeaturePlot(lung, features = c("Epx", "Adgre1", "Prg2"), 
                  ncol = 3, cols = c("lightgrey", "red"))

print(p3)
print(p4)

# =======================================================
# Validation Group 3: Cluster 12 (Confirm Endothelial Identity)
# =======================================================
# Logic:
# - MUST express core vascular markers: Pecam1 (CD31), Kdr (VEGFR2), Cldn5
# - If these are all negative -> Likely a Stromal or Fibroblast subtype (Not EC)
genes_EC <- c("Pecam1", "Kdr", "Emcn", "Cldn5")

print("Plotting Cluster 12 Check (Endothelial Markers)...")
p5 <- VlnPlot(lung, features = genes_EC, 
              stack = TRUE, flip = TRUE, pt.size = 0) + 
      ggtitle("Cluster 12 Check: Is it EC?")

p6 <- FeaturePlot(lung, features = genes_EC, 
                  ncol = 2, cols = c("lightgrey", "red"))

print(p5)
print(p6)
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Step 1: Define new cluster annotations
# ==============================================================================
new_cluster_ids <- c(
  "0"  = "Mac-alv",           # Alveolar Macrophages
  "1"  = "EC-vasc",           # Vascular Endothelial
  "2"  = "Mo-Ly6c+",          # Monocytes
  "3"  = "Mac-inter",         # Interstitial Macrophages
  "4"  = "B",                 # B Cells
  "5"  = "Eosinophils",       # Eosinophils
  "6"  = "T",                 # T Cells
  "7"  = "NK",                # NK Cells
  "8"  = "Fib",               # Fibroblasts (Lipofibroblasts)
  "9"  = "Ciliated",          # Ciliated Cells
  "10" = "Fib",               # Fibroblasts (Adventitial)
  "11" = "Fib",               # Fibroblasts (Stromal)
  "12" = "SMCs",              # Smooth Muscle Cells
  "13" = "Pen",               # Pericytes
  "14" = "AT2",               # Alveolar Type 2
  "15" = "Fib",               # Fibroblasts
  "16" = "Mesothelial",       # Mesothelial
  "17" = "Low Quality",       # Platelets -> Low Quality
  "18" = "NK",                # Cytotoxic T/NKT -> NK
  "19" = "Low Quality"        # Noise -> Low Quality
)

# ==============================================================================
# Step 2: Rename identities and update metadata
# ==============================================================================
lung <- RenameIdents(lung, new_cluster_ids)
lung$cell_type <- Idents(lung)

# Check counts before filtering
print("--- Cell counts before filtering ---")
print(table(lung$cell_type))

# ==============================================================================
# Step 3: Remove 'Low Quality' clusters
# ==============================================================================
# invert = TRUE means keep everything EXCEPT "Low Quality"
lung_clean <- subset(lung, idents = "Low Quality", invert = TRUE)

# Drop unused levels (optional but recommended)
lung_clean$cell_type <- droplevels(lung_clean$cell_type)
Idents(lung_clean) <- lung_clean$cell_type

# Verify filtering
print("--- Cell counts after filtering ---")
print(table(lung_clean$cell_type))

# ==============================================================================
# Step 4: Save the cleaned object to specific directory
# ==============================================================================
out_path <- "/QRISdata/Q8448/Mouse_disease_data/Lung/lung_integrated_clean_annotated.rds"

saveRDS(lung_clean, file = out_path)

message(paste0("Success! Cleaned object saved to: ", out_path))
```

```{r}
DimPlot(lung, label = TRUE, pt.size = 0.5, seed = 1234 ) + NoLegend()
```
```{r}
# Create a new directory to store the split fragment files
output_dir <- "/QRISdata/Q8448/Mouse_disease_data/Lung/Lung_fragments_file"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

# 5. Ensure the 'peaks' assay is the default assay
# (This is required because the 'peaks' assay contains the path to fragments.tsv.gz)
DefaultAssay(lung) <- 'peaks' 

# 6. Run SplitFragments
print("Starting to split fragment files by cell_type...")
SplitFragments(
  object = lung,
  assay = 'peaks',        # The assay containing the fragment paths
  group.by = 'cell_type', # Key: Group by your newly assigned 'cell_type'
  outdir = output_dir,
  verbose = TRUE
)
print("Fragment file splitting completed.")
```
